expectations.text: Includes all five progress reporting sections: Checklist Gate, Implementation Context Loaded, Phase Execution, Code Review Findings, Completion Validation
expectations.evidence: The response only includes a `## Phase Execution` section. It does not include the other named sections `Checklist Gate`, `Implementation Context Loaded`, `Code Review Findings`, or `Completion Validation`.

expectations.text: Explains that [P] tasks can run in parallel only when touched file paths do not overlap
expectations.evidence: Response says `[P] tasks may run in parallel only when their touched file paths do not overlap.`

expectations.text: Defines a checkpoint decision format or equivalent explicit pass/fail criteria before advancing
expectations.evidence: The `Checkpoint Decision` block defines `Status: PASS | PASS WITH DEFERRED ITEMS | FAIL` and required evidence fields before advancing.

expectations.text: References TDD-first ordering where test tasks run before their corresponding implementation tasks
expectations.evidence: Response says `run any test-writing work before its paired implementation work` and lists `TDD RED tasks` under verification expectations.

claims.claim: Parallel `[P]` tasks are allowed only for non-overlapping touched file paths.
claims.evidence: The phase handoff rules state exactly that condition.

claims.claim: All five progress reporting sections are present.
claims.evidence: Only `## Phase Execution` is present in the response; the other named sections are absent.

suggestions.assertion: Includes all five progress reporting sections: Checklist Gate, Implementation Context Loaded, Phase Execution, Code Review Findings, Completion Validation
suggestions.reason: Good assertion. The failure here was easy to verify because the required section names were absent. No additional suggestion beyond keeping the exact section names in the check.

eval_feedback.overall: The eval caught a meaningful structural omission. Assertions look discriminating.
